message:
feat(core,abi): port ns_name_skip + uncompressed_p completing ns_name_* family in core (bd-nss-epic)

Lifts the remaining ns_name_* helpers — ns_name_skip (advances past
one wire-format name), __ns_name_skip (glibc-internal duplicate), and
__ns_name_uncompressed_p (predicate: no compression pointers in the
name) — from glibc_internal_abi.rs into core::resolv::dns_name.

New core surface (in resolv::dns_name):
  pub fn name_skip(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<usize, NameError>
  pub fn is_uncompressed(buf: &[u8]) -> bool

name_skip walks labels until root or a compression pointer (the
latter is treated as a single 2-byte unit and NOT followed),
returning total bytes consumed. is_uncompressed returns true iff the
name in buf reaches the root terminator with no high-bit-set label-
length byte (catches 0xC0 compression pointers AND 0x40/0x80 RFC 2671
reserved label types).

abi changes (in glibc_internal_abi.rs):
  - 3 inline impls (~95 lines combined) replaced with thin shims.
  - Drop orphan const NS_CMPRSFLGS (now in core).

Combined with bd-nsn-epic and bd-nsu-epic, the entire ns_name_*
family (10+ entry points: ntop, pton, unpack, pack, skip,
uncompressed_p — each with __ glibc-internal duplicate where
applicable) now delegates to a single set of core helpers.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-nss-2):
  - 16 new core::resolv::dns_name tests pass (8 name_skip +
    8 is_uncompressed cases including pointer-at-end-of-name,
    truncated-pointer, extended-label rejection)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 29/29 ns_name integration tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-nss-epic.
